original_hypothesis.title: Manager Can Drain Reserved Withdrawal Assets via Arbitrary manage() Calls
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The manage() function allows the manager to make arbitrary external calls from the vault, including ERC20 transfers of the underlying token. There is no check that the vault retains at least reservedWithdrawalAssets worth of underlying tokens after the call. A compromised manager can drain all assets including those reserved for already-fulfilled withdrawals, making executeWithdrawal permanently revert for all users.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While the manager is trusted by design, a single compromised key can drain the entire vault including assets that users have already been promised (fulfilled withdrawals). The lack of any minimum balance enforcement creates a single point of failure.

proof_of_signal.pattern: target.functionCallWithValue(data, value) with no restriction on target or data; can transfer underlying token out

preconditions: Manager private key compromised or manager acts maliciously
Vault holds underlying tokens including reservedWithdrawalAssets

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Unrestricted arbitrary call with no post-condition check on vault balance
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No minimum balance enforcement; no restriction on calling underlying token

analysis_questions: Could manage() be restricted to prevent underlying token transfers beyond a threshold?
Is there a way to enforce reservedWithdrawalAssets as a minimum balance?
What is the trust model for the manager key?
validation_requirements: Call manage to transfer all underlying tokens out
Verify executeWithdrawal reverts for all users afterward
Confirm users who burned shares for withdrawal have no recovery

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Manager key is compromised. Attacker calls manage(underlyingToken, abi.encodeWithSelector(IERC20.transfer.selector, attacker, vault.balance), 0). All underlying tokens are drained. Users with fulfilled but unexecuted withdrawals call executeWithdrawal which reverts on SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer due to insufficient balance. Their shares were already burned during requestWithdrawal, so they have no recourse.
prerequisites: Manager key compromise or malicious manager

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Total loss of all vault assets including reserved withdrawal assets
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All vault users; worst for those with fulfilled but unexecuted withdrawals (shares already burned)

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing test verifying manage() cannot drain reserved assets

technical_investigation_focus.capital_requirements: N/A - attacker gains all vault assets

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
